etymology.description: semantic 耳(耳) (ěr, "ear") + phonetic 殸(殸) -> perception of sounds
etymology.interpretation: The character 聲 (shēng, "sound"; "voice") combines 耳(耳) (ěr, "ear") as the semantic component, suggesting listening or hearing, with 殸(殸) as the phonetic component, to produce the concept of a sound as perceived by the ear.
etymology.simplification: 声 is a simplified form where the character 殸 has been reduced to 士(士) (shì, "scholar") plus the addition of 𠂇 (movement), dropping the 耳(耳) (ěr, "ear") component for simplicity.

examples.chinese: 我听到了一种奇怪的声音(我聽到了一種奇怪的聲音)。
examples.pinyin: Wǒ tīngdào le yī zhǒng qíguài de shēngyīn.

examples.chinese: 鸟儿在树上快乐地歌唱(鳥兒在樹上快樂地歌唱)，它们的声音如音乐般动听(它們的聲音如音樂般動聽)。
examples.pinyin: Niǎor men zài shù shàng kuàilè de gēchàng, tāmen de shēngyīn rú yīnyuè bān dòngtīng.
examples.english: The birds sing happily on the tree, and their voices are as melodious as music.

examples.chinese: 老师的声音充满了自信(老師的聲音充滿了自信)，让学生们感受到安全(讓學生們感受到安全)。
examples.pinyin: Lǎoshī de shēngyīn chōngmǎnle zìxìn, ràng xuéshēngmen gǎnshòudào ānquán.
examples.english: The teacher's voice was full of confidence, making the students feel safe.
